# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant the account 'roles/owner' at project level so it can manage all GCP resources without permission errors. This enables full automation of our infrastructure deployments.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# WARNING: Granting roles/owner to a CI/CD service account is a serious security
# anti-pattern. It violates the Principle of Least Privilege and creates
# significant risk: credential compromise would give an attacker FULL control
# of your project, including billing, IAM, and data access.
#
# The template below follows security best practices by granting only the
# specific roles needed for typical CI/CD infrastructure deployments.
# Add or remove roles based on your actual requirements.

resources:
  - name: cicd-pipeline-service-account
    type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
    properties:
      accountId: cicd-pipeline-sa
      displayName: CI/CD Pipeline Service Account
      description: >
        Service account for CI/CD pipeline with least-privilege permissions.
        Do NOT grant roles/owner - scope permissions to what is actually needed.

  - name: cicd-role-compute-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/compute.admin
      member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
    metadata:
      dependsOn:
        - cicd-pipeline-service-account

  - name: cicd-role-container-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/container.admin
      member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
    metadata:
      dependsOn:
        - cicd-pipeline-service-account

  - name: cicd-role-storage-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/storage.admin
      member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
    metadata:
      dependsOn:
        - cicd-pipeline-service-account

  - name: cicd-role-cloudsql-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/cloudsql.admin
      member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
    metadata:
      dependsOn:
        - cicd-pipeline-service-account

  - name: cicd-role-cloudbuild-editor
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/cloudbuild.builds.editor
      member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
    metadata:
      dependsOn:
        - cicd-pipeline-service-account

  - name: cicd-role-serviceusage-consumer
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/serviceusage.serviceUsageConsumer
      member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
    metadata:
      dependsOn:
        - cicd-pipeline-service-account

  # Add roles/deploymentmanager.editor if this SA needs to run DM deployments
  - name: cicd-role-dm-editor
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: {{ env["project"] }}
      role: roles/deploymentmanager.editor
      member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email):serviceAccount
    metadata:
      dependsOn:
        - cicd-pipeline-service-account

outputs:
  - name: serviceAccountEmail
    value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)
  - name: securityNote
    value: >
      This service account uses least-privilege roles scoped to CI/CD needs.
      If you encounter permission errors, identify the specific permission
      required and add only that role - do NOT escalate to roles/owner.
      Review granted roles periodically and remove any that are unused.
